summary: Average length of stay for families with children in shelter fell sharply from FY22 to FY24 as a large wave of recently arrived asylum-seeking families — who by definition had short stays so far — pulled the average down, alongside faster housing placements. The FY25 uptick reversed part of that: DHS's preliminary FY26 report explains that as fewer new families entered shelter, the mix shifted toward longer-tenured households, which mechanically raises the average length of stay even without anyone individually staying longer.

sources.label: DHS, Preliminary Mayor's Management Report, Fiscal 2026
